title: Sync & External Scripts
summary: Updated 4 external sync scripts to use unified n5_core.db database

artifacts_modified.change: Updated to use db_paths import, get_db_connection() for unified DB, added log_deal_activity(), ensure_person_exists() functions
Full rewrite to use unified DB. Uses people+deal_roles instead of deal_contacts, creates interactions entries instead of processed_emails
Full rewrite to use unified DB. Creates people entries, deal_roles, and organizations. Sync functions updated for new schema
Added unified DB imports while maintaining legacy deals.db for outbox operations. Imports db_paths module

verification_results.sms_deal_handler_dry_run: PASS - Returns 'No deal found' for TestCo (expected)

notes_for_orchestrator: notion_deal_sync.py maintains dual-database approach: unified DB for reads, legacy deals.db for outbox. This preserves existing Notion sync functionality while enabling unified queries.
email_deal_scanner now creates interactions entries instead of processed_emails table (which doesn't exist in unified schema). This is semantically equivalent.

All scripts now import from db_paths.py for unified database access. Functions like ensure_person_exists() and create_deal_role() are duplicated across scripts - could be extracted to a shared utils module in future refactor.
sms_deal_handler still accepts --db-path argument for backward compatibility but defaults to unified DB.
gaps_or_warnings: notion_deal_sync DealDB class still operates on legacy deals.db - full migration would require more extensive changes
Duplicate helper functions (ensure_person_exists, create_deal_role) across multiple scripts could be consolidated
